# fix(diagnostics): PR#570 review findings — remove field re-export, add sort_keys test, JudgeWinner alias, doc-strength

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `da3dc211dbf9` — fix(diagnostics): PR#570 review findings — remove field re-export, add sort_keys test, JudgeWinner alias, doc-strength

**Body**:

Addresses all 2 MED + 6 LOW findings from the MUST-gate reviewer +
security-reviewer runs on PR #570.

**Code Review MED #1 — Remove `field` re-export pollution**

protocols.py appended `field` to __all__ but __init__.py did not re-export
it. Inconsistent surface. Removed the 10-line re-export block and the
now-unused `field` import; the class-level docstring already told adapters
to `from dataclasses import field` directly.

**Code Review MED #2 — Add sort_keys regression test**

New `test_fingerprint_identical_under_dict_insertion_order` constructs two
TraceEvents with payloads in different dict insertion orders and asserts
identical fingerprints. This is the structural guard that a future
accidental `sort_keys=False` refactor would trip — previously only
`test_fingerprint_deterministic` covered same-input determinism, which
wouldn't have caught that class of drift.

**Code Review LOW — JudgeWinner Literal type alias**

Added `JudgeWinner = Optional[Literal["A", "B", "tie"]]` alongside the
existing runtime `_VALID_WINNERS = frozenset({...})` check. Gives
mypy/pyright static enforcement at assignment sites; the frozenset
stays as defense-in-depth at wire boundaries (from_dict). Exported
from kailash.diagnostics.__init__.__all__. New test class
`TestJudgeWinnerTypeAlias` exercises the alias.

**Code Review LOW — Error-message set-ordering**

`JudgeResult.__post_init__` error message was `f"must be one of {'A',
'B', 'tie', None}, ..."` — a set literal whose repr order is
non-deterministic across runs. Changed to explicit tuple literal
`"must be one of ('A', 'B', 'tie', None), ..."`. Cosmetic; pins
error-message determinism so regression tests can match on substring
without flake.

**Security Review LOW #1 — tenant_id SHOULD → MUST**

Per rules/tenant-isolation.md §§4-5, when tenant_id is populated,
downstream sinks MUST partition storage/metrics/audit rows by tenant
(not SHOULD). Strengthened both the protocols.py class docstring and
the schemas/trace-event.v1.json property description. `None` remains
permitted only for single-tenant deployments.

**Security Review LOW #2 — Payload redaction MUST visible at Python
dataclass level**

The schema's `payload` property description had the emitter-
responsibility MUST; the Python class docstring only said "kept opaque."
Adapter authors reading the Python source would miss the rule.
Copied the "Emitters MUST apply rules/event-payload-classification.md
rules..." clause verbatim into both the TraceEvent class docstring
Optional-fields section AND the inline comment above the `payload`
field definition.

**Security Review LOW #3 — payload_hash 8-hex rationale inline**

The payload_hash docstring used to defer to `rules/event-payload-
classification.md §2` without stating the entropy rationale. Restated
inline: "Eight hex chars (32 bits of entropy) is the cross-SDK
contract: sufficient for forensic correlation across event + log + DB
audit streams, insufficient for rainbow-table reversal of typical PK
strings." A Python reader no longer needs to follow a rule link to
learn why 8 and not 16/64.

**Verification**

tests/unit/diagnostics/test_protocols.py: 35 passed (up from 33 — 2
new regression tests). Pre-commit hooks pass.

Related: kailash-py #567 / #569 / #570, esperie/kailash-rs#449.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
